title: Fold-in outside display mirror
abstract: An external rearview assembly includes a housing pivotally coupled with a vehicle door and operable between an extended position and a retracted position. An electro-optic element is operably coupled with the housing. A display module is disposed proximate the electro-optic element. The display module is activated when the housing is in the retracted position. The display module is deactivated when the housing is in the extended position. An imager is operably coupled with the housing and is configured to capture image data when the housing is in the retracted position and display the image data on the activated display module.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. An external rearview assembly comprising: a housing pivotally coupled with a vehicle door and operable between an extended position and a retracted position; an electro-optic element operably coupled with the housing; a display module disposed proximate the electro-optic element, wherein the display module is activated when the housing is in the retracted position, and wherein the display module is deactivated when the housing is in the extended position; and an imager operably coupled with the housing and configured to capture image data when the housing is in the retracted position and display the image data on the activated display module. 2. The external rearview assembly of claim 1, wherein the imager is disposed within the housing and is in optical communication with an imager lens positioned on the housing. 3. The external rearview assembly of either of claims 1 or 2, wherein the imager is adjustable to provide a rearward field of view greater than or less than a field of view provided by the electro-optic element. 4. The external rearview assembly of any of claims 1-3, wherein the electro-optic element is configured to darken when the housing is in the extended position and lighten when the housing is in the retracted position. 5. The external rearview assembly of any of claims 1-4, wherein the electro-optic element and the display module are nested within a carrier plate inside the housing. 6. The external rearview assembly of claim 5, wherein the imager includes a lens barrel adjacent to a peripheral edge of the carrier plate. 7. The external rearview assembly of any of claims 1-6, further comprising: 

 a circuit board operably coupled with the imager and configured to activate and deactivate the imager. 8. The external rearview assembly of claim 7, further comprising: a heater disposed between the circuit board and the electro-optic element. 9. The external rearview assembly of claim 8, wherein the heater extends across substantially all of a back side of the display module and the circuit board. 10. An external rearview assembly for a vehicle comprising: a housing including a proximal end pivotally coupled with a vehicle door, the housing being operable between an extended position and a retracted position; an electro-optic element operably coupled with the housing; a display module disposed proximate the electro-optic element, wherein the display module is activated when the housing is in the retracted position, and wherein the display module is deactivated when the housing is in the extended position; and an imager operably disposed on a distal end of the housing and configured to capture image data behind said vehicle when the housing is in the retracted position. 11. The external rearview assembly of claim 10, wherein the electro-optic element is configured to darken when the housing is in the extended position and lighten when the housing is in the retracted position. 12. The external rearview assembly of either of claims 10 or 11, wherein the electro- optic element and the display module are nested within a carrier plate inside the housing. 13. The external rearview assembly of any of claims 10-12, further comprising: a circuit board operably coupled with the imager and configured to activate and deactivate the imager. 14. The external rearview assembly of claim 13, further comprising: 

 a heater disposed between the circuit board and the electro-optic element. 15. An external rearview assembly comprising: a housing pivotally coupled with a vehicle door and operable between an extended position and a retracted position; an electro-optic element operably coupled with the housing; a display module disposed proximate the electro-optic element, wherein the display module is activated when the housing is in the retracted position, and wherein the display module is deactivated when the housing is in the extended position; and an imager configured to capture image data and display the image data on the activated display module. 16. The external rearview assembly of claim 15, wherein the imager is disposed within the housing and is in optical communication with an imager lens positioned on the housing. 17. The external rearview assembly of either of claims 15 or 16, wherein the imager is adjustable to provide a rearward field of view greater than or less than a field of view provided by the electro-optic element. 18. The external rearview assembly of any of claims 15-17, further comprising: a circuit board operably coupled with the imager and configured to activate and deactivate the imager. 19. The external rearview assembly of claim 18, further comprising: a heater disposed between the circuit board and the electro-optic element. 20. The external rearview assembly of claim 19, wherein the heater extends across substantially all of a back side of the display module and the circuit board.
